mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and lowers equity discount rates, directly compressing valuation multiples and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, triggering negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances; t-stat for return difference versus baseline falls below 1.5 across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases; interaction term coefficient remains statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during an inflationary supply shock regime reflects severe capacity constraints and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors price in stagflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually; out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation combined with relatively low policy rates signals delayed monetary tightening and elevated stagflation risk, compressing real yields and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when real yields remain positive; walk-forward Sharpe ratio of a signal-weighted portfolio remains below 0.0 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls supports aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings guidance improves.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple market cycles; Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth coupled with declining retail sales confirms a broad macroeconomic demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as equity analysts downgrade earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not outperform a buy-and-hold strategy during confirmed NBER recessions; false positive rate exceeds 45% in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production signals improving manufacturing utilization and easing supply bottlenecks, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as margin expansion expectations rise.
failure_condition: INDPRO spikes fail to generate positive 5-day returns when accompanied by rising inventory-to-sales ratios; signal directional accuracy remains below 50% over a 5-year backtest.
